name: Strawberry Basket
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour an 11" x 5-inch loaf pan; set aside.
On a sheet of waxed paper combine the sifted flour, sugar, baking powder, and 1/2 teaspoon salt; set aside.
In a large bowl at high speed beat the heavy cream until stiff; set aside.
In a medium bowl at high speed beat the eggs and vanilla extract until thick and lemon-colored; fold into the whipped cream. With a spatula, fold in the flour mixture and pour into the pan.
Bake for 45-50 minutes. Cool on a wire rack.
Cut off 1-inch slice from top of cake. Using a knife with a serrated edge, carefully remove the cake to create a shell with 1-inch walls.
Fill the cake shell with strawberry mousse (see recipe below).
Cover the cake with icing (see recipe below); garnish with strawberries.
Place the remaining icing in a pastry bag fitted with a weave tip; decorate the sides of the cake with a basket pattern. Using a star tip, use the remaining icing to decorate the top edge of the cake.
Strawberry Mousse
1 Envelope Unflavored Gelatin
1/4 Cup Amaretto
1 Cup Heavy Cream
1 Pint Strawberries, Sliced
1/2 Cup Sugar
In a small glass measure, sprinkle the gelatin over the amaretto and set aside for 5 minutes until the gelatin softens. In a small bowl at high speed beat the heavy cream until stiff; refrigerate.
In a large skillet over low heat, toss the strawberries with sugar until the sugar dissolves (about 3 minutes). Remove from heat, stir in the gelatin mixture until blended. Cool to room temperature; fold into the whipped cream.
Icing:
2 Cups Heavy Cream
1/4 Cup Confectioner's Sugar
2 Teaspoons Vanilla Extract
In a large bowl at high speed beat the ingredients until the mixture is stiff.
